g.title: cluster_store
g.text: store/ — the only place SQL is written

g.title: cluster_gs
g.text: gradescope/ — all undocumented-API fragility is quarantined here

g.title: cluster_sync
g.text: sync/ — orchestration; prune is a pure function over in-memory bytes

g.title: cluster_prov
g.text: provenance/ — the only module that knows Provenance's HTTP shape

g.title: loop
g.text: each one is a try/except island with its own
runs row — one outage never aborts the others

g.title: fern
g.text: exactly one seam; plaintext exists only
transiently, in memory, during a pass

g.title: prune
g.text: include only folders not in the watermark;

g.title: never
g.text: the YAML — a round-trip risks silently dropping

g.title: post
g.text: No assignment id is sent — Provenance derives it

g.title: adv
g.text: only now, after succeeded or partial

g.title: hold
g.text: so the next run retries. A crash mid-poll must

g.title: dedupnote
g.text: Provenance dedups by content hash before any heavy work.
The watermark is therefore an OPTIMISATION, never the correctness
mechanism — when in doubt, forward. A resubmission gets a new
Gradescope key and must be sent; if the watermark gated that,
a changed submission could be silently lost.

g.title: runs
g.text: class label, counts, job ids, outcomes —
never a password, token or cookie

g.title: notify
g.text: fires only after the pass is already reported,
